description:
You are the release-manager for a small distributed system.  
        All service health probes write to individual log files that already exist under  
        /home/user/distributed-logs/ .  
        
        Goal  
        ----  
        1. Inspect every file whose name ends in “.log” inside /home/user/distributed-logs/ .  
        2. Find every line that contains the exact text:  
              “HealthCheck: FAILED”  
        3. For each such line, capture the service name that appears immediately after the word “Service”.  
           Example (do **not** hard-code):  
               “[2023-07-01 10:10:00] Service frontend: HealthCheck: FAILED” → service name = “frontend”  
        4. Build a unique, alphabetically-sorted list of all service names that ever reported a **FAILED** health-check.  
        5. Create the directory /home/user/deployment/ if it does not already exist and write the list to a new text file called  
           /home/user/deployment/release_blockers.log  
           • Each line must begin with exactly the literal text “BLOCKER: ” (including the space).  
           • Then write the service name.  
           • Do not include any extra characters, timestamps, or trailing spaces.  
           • There must be **one terminating newline** at the end of the file and **no blank lines** elsewhere.  
           Correct final example format (order is alphabetical and lines are unique):  
               BLOCKER: backend  
               BLOCKER: frontend  
               BLOCKER: payments  
           
        Verification notes  
        ------------------  
        The automated checker will:  
        • Confirm that /home/user/deployment/release_blockers.log exists.  
        • Read every line and verify it matches the pattern ^BLOCKER:\s[a-z0-9_-]+$  
        • Confirm the set of service names exactly matches the FAILED services present in the logs and is alphabetically sorted.  
        • Ensure there is exactly one newline character at the end of the file and no extra blank lines.  
        
        Hints (you may ignore): a single grep/awk/uniq/sort pipeline is enough, but any method is acceptable as long as the output file format is **exactly** as described.
truth:
Initial files (already present before the agent starts)  
        ------------------------------------------------------  
        1. /home/user/distributed-logs/frontend.log  
           Contents:  
           [2023-07-01 10:00:00] Service frontend: HealthCheck: OK  
           [2023-07-01 10:05:00] Service frontend: HealthCheck: OK  
           [2023-07-01 10:10:00] Service frontend: HealthCheck: FAILED  
           [2023-07-01 10:15:00] Service frontend: HealthCheck: OK  
           
        2. /home/user/distributed-logs/backend.log  
           Contents:  
           [2023-07-01 10:00:00] Service backend: HealthCheck: OK  
           [2023-07-01 10:05:00] Service backend: HealthCheck: FAILED  
           [2023-07-01 10:10:00] Service backend: HealthCheck: FAILED  
           
        3. /home/user/distributed-logs/auth.log  
           Contents:  
           [2023-07-01 10:00:00] Service auth: HealthCheck: OK  
           [2023-07-01 10:05:00] Service auth: HealthCheck: OK  
           
        4. /home/user/distributed-logs/payments.log  
           Contents:  
           [2023-07-01 10:00:00] Service payments: HealthCheck: FAILED  
           [2023-07-01 10:05:00] Service payments: HealthCheck: OK  
           
        5. /home/user/distributed-logs/cache.log  
           Contents:  
           [2023-07-01 10:00:00] Service cache: HealthCheck: OK  
           [2023-07-01 10:05:00] Service cache: HealthCheck: OK  
           
        Files/directories that MUST exist after successful task completion  
        -----------------------------------------------------------------  
        1. /home/user/deployment/          (directory)  
             • Must be creatable without root privileges; default user permissions are fine.  
               
        2. /home/user/deployment/release_blockers.log  
           Exact contents (including a single trailing newline character):  
           BLOCKER: backend  
           BLOCKER: frontend  
           BLOCKER: payments  
           
        No other modifications are required or will be checked.
